TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Atayal, story: AtaNr-moving_Kainu

S.FORM: kalanay mu ku sami ka matu'uwal de nake a kinqiyanux.
S.TRANSL: 我來敘說我們Matu'uwal 的生活。
I will tell you our life of Matu'uwal.

S.FORM: aring su nabakis sunga' ga ini' mu baqi ku kinqiyanux nku nabakis gi ti'i kuwing hani ga a kinabahan kuwing sawni' la.
S.TRANSL: 從老人家以前，我不知道老人家的生活，因為我已經是現代人。
Since the forefathers, I don’t know their lives because I live in modern days.

S.FORM: ru tiku inkahulan ga ini' baqi asikina ga pinugan sku kai' nku nakautas miyan ka aruwa masisuru i aruwa i babaw na kaal la.
S.TRANSL: 我不知道我們的發源地，但我聽已經去世的長輩們說過。
I don’t know our origin, but I’ve heard about it from our ancestors.

S.FORM: tiku inuwahan miyan ga minaki sami i papak hasa.
S.TRANSL: 我們的發源地和住過的地方是Papak。
The place we came from and the place we used to live in is Papak.

S.FORM: maki sami papak sunga' ga ini' sami kahabag gi nanu' gi itaal sami ga ki sami minukahul inu’ ga ini' baqi.
S.TRANSL: 我們以前住在Papak的時候，人不多，因為我們泰雅人連從哪裡來都不知道。
When we lived in Papak, there were not manu people because we even don’t know where we came from.

S.FORM: ru nanu' u yani ku a niyan mu i sikaal.
S.TRANSL: 這就是我正在敘述的事情。
This is what I am saying.

S.FORM: kinqiyanux nku gipun ka niyal i a mararaya sku rauq ku wasilung ga.
S.TRANSL: 日本時代的生活，河水上升淹沒了土地。
During the life under Japanese occupation, the water increased and the flood engulfed the land.

S.FORM: mkgi ru mgiyay sami ki papak mgiyay sami kiya la ga kiya ruma' la ga aruwa i qaluysun na wasilung la ru ini' kahabag la ku maki kiya.
S.TRANSL: 我們離開Papak之後，經洪水沖刷，現在沒有很多人住那邊了。
After we left Papak, there were not many people living there after being washed away by the flood.

S.FORM: ki'i mahananu ku kinqiyanux miyan ka niya sami lahuwun na kamisama’ ruwa.
S.TRANSL: 不知道怎麼回事，老天仍留他們繼續在那邊生活。
Not knowing how, the gods keep them living there.

S.FORM: aruwa la ku wasilung la gi aruwa mayayahu la ku wasilung la gi e ki'i yani ku ska' nia la ru aruwa i mana'aping la ku wasilung la ru muwah sami la i mabatuan hani la.
S.TRANSL: 或許已經夠了，洪水就退了，水乾了之後我們就來到Mabatuan。
When the flood went away, perhaps because (the gods think) it is enough, the water dried and we came to Mabatuan.

S.FORM: muwah sami mabatuan ru ukas ku niqun niyam la ga.
S.TRANSL: 我們來到Mabatuan之後沒有糧食。
When we came to Mabatuan, there was no food.

S.FORM: naniqun niyam la ga niyan lumanglung ku na sami ka nabakis maha u kiya ta' tmubuh tikay su naniqun.
S.TRANSL: 沒有糧食，我們的老人家就想說，我們來播種一些糧食作物。
There is no food, so our forefathers thought that we can sow and grow some food.

S.FORM: nanu' u musa' sami i kumiyum sku gaghap ru lumuwag sami sku gaghap gaghap na pati' aping.
S.TRANSL: 我們去尋找而且找到了種子，如旱稻的種子。
We go to look for seeds and find seeds like seeds of dry rice.

S.FORM: ru i makabuway la ga yani ku a kalhun miyan suxu'un miyan na luhiyung.
S.TRANSL: 稻子結穗之後，我們收割並且用杵臼搗米。
After the rice bear seeds, we harvest and pound the rice with mortars.

S.FORM: gi kiya ku luhiyung sunga' ka kinbalay miyan nanak la ru.
S.TRANSL: 因為以前(只)有臼，我們都得自己做。
Because there was mortar before, we have to make it by ourselves.

S.FORM: suxu'un miyan kiya gi bali kiya a tingki bali kiya a a nanu' nanak bay ku qahba' bay ku asi langlung gi ini' i qabaq su inlalungan ku nabakis.
S.TRANSL: 沒有電，而且老人家想不到別的方法，只能用手搗米。
There was no electricity, and the forefathers could not think of other ways but using hands to pound the rice.

S.FORM: ru suxu'un miyan sa luhiyung ru matababuwax la ku pati' ku buwah la ga.
S.TRANSL: 我們用臼將稻穀脫殼搗成白米。
We pound the rice and turn unhusked rice into uncooked rice with the mortar.

S.FORM: nanu' u pahapuyun miyan pahapuyun miyan la ru yani ku sikaqiyanux miyan.
S.TRANSL: 我們煮飯，這就是我們生活的方式。
And we cooked, and that is how we live our lives.

S.FORM: ru tiku hani la ga niyan sami i maki sami mabatuan sunga' ga.
S.TRANSL: 之後我們就住在Mabatuan。
Afterwards, we live in Mabatuan.

S.FORM: kumaal ku gipun sunga' maha u gi tiku gipun sunga’ ga minlahang tikay ku taiwan ru tiku gipun ga kumaal maha uwah bay hugal la ru psibaqay simu su paptauwaw la maha.
S.TRANSL: 日本人治理台灣時，他們說如果我們搬下來，他們會教我們耕種。
When the Japanese ruled Taiwan, they said that if we move downward, they would teach us how to farm.

S.FORM: nanu' muwah sami subalay tagiruni la gi tiku kakian miyan hani ga sabawqil salun.
S.TRANSL: 我們真的搬下來到這，我們現在住的地方叫做 Sabawqil。
We did move downward here, and the place we're staying now is called Sabawqil.

S.FORM: ru mabatuan san gi suwa' salun mabatuan ga inkahulan miyan sku inkahulan miyan sku inkahulan miyan i mabatuan mabatuan salun ku rauq ka yani.
S.TRANSL: 為什麼叫Mabatuan是因為我們的祖居地，我們發源的那塊土地就叫Mabatuan。
As for why it is called Mabatuan, it’s because the place we came from, our origin, was the land called Mabatuan.

S.FORM: ru muwah sami runi i i tapin hani.
S.TRANSL: 接著我們來到大坪這邊。
Then we come to Tapin.

S.FORM: ru ukas bay ku a naniqun miyan.
S.TRANSL: 而且我們真的沒有糧食。
And we really have nothing to eat.

S.FORM: kumaal ku gipun maha u psibaqay simu su a kinqiyanux naki simu baq ma nanu' u kaihu nanak su slaq ma.
S.TRANSL: 日本人說，我們會教你們耕種，這樣你們才知道如何墾自己的田來過生活。
The Japanese say, “we will teach you farming so that you know how to farm your own land.”

S.FORM: nanu' tiku slaq ka nangi' na e piang piang tikay hugal hasa ga.
S.TRANSL: 下面那一點平平的地。
The pieces of flat land down below.

S.FORM: nanu' usa' kinhiwan bay na qaba' bali kiya ku kakaihu sunga' bay ukas a a kuwaisho la haishi sheme nake chichi ye meyo la.
S.TRANSL: 去挖地時，我們真的用手，因為沒有挖土機，也沒有什麼機器。
When we go digging the land, we use hands because there is no excavator or machine.

S.FORM: a e swoi yani ku niya mu lungulungun sku hani ru atu' na aqih bay ku kinqiyanux miyan ka salun maha u mabatuan.
S.TRANSL: 我真的覺得我們這個叫做大坪的地方，生活真的很不好。
I truly think our lives in the place called Mabatuan is not really good.

S.FORM: shish i ye ciangde putsuo haish zenmeyang wome ye wowo ye pusiaude la.
S.TRANSL: 事實上這樣講也沒錯，反正怎樣我是不曉得啦。
Actually, such saying may be right; anyway, I am not quite sure.

S.FORM: yinwe kuwing ka nabakis la ga ini' mu baqi i panaspung ku inlulungan nku malahang ita'.
S.TRANSL: 像我這個老人家也無法評斷照顧我們的人(政府)的想法。
Old people like me cannot comment on the thoughts of those who take care of us (the government).

S.FORM: nanu' yani ku niya kung maha u sami ka itaal ka niya' i mabatuan na hani aqih bay kinqiyanux miyan.
S.TRANSL: 所以我說，我們在Mabatuan的生活真的不好。
So I say our lives in Mabatuan are relly not good.

S.FORM: e ulung yaku tawki' ka niya i e pasibaq su kai' sami maha u.
S.TRANSL: 幸好有政府在指導我們生活方法。
Luckily, the government is teaching us how to live.

S.FORM: a kalawkah matauwaw ru pumuwa' su anananu' shueikuo la haiyo nake yumi sheme nochanpin ga i a nimenyauco gi nimen meyo co ga nimen huei osh maha ku malahang ita' ru.
S.TRANSL: 政府說你們要努力工作，種各種作物，像水果、玉米等農產品，你們要工作，因為如果不工作就會餓死。
The government says that you have to work hard and grow all kinds of produce like fruits and corn. You have to work or you will starve to die.

S.FORM: e bali i itaal sami ukas bay ku baqun miyan gi ki'i sunga' hiya' ga ma'imag sami na ss sami sku qasinu ruwa.
S.TRANSL: 我們有很多事情不懂，可能是因為我們泰雅人以前和野獸住一起。
We don’t know much probably because we Atayal (used to) live with wild animals.

S.FORM: kiya ruma' ga magayungay ru magabauwak ru.
S.TRANSL: 有些變成猴子，有些變成山豬。
Some turn into monkeys and some become boars.

S.FORM: ini' i sami pakababaq la ru siancai la ga u kiya ku e kai' nku muyu ka maha pasibaqay sami su kai' la ru naki simu i e baq su kai' naki simu i b baq naki miyan baqun i mung ku kai' mamu maha ka ku.
S.TRANSL: 我們以前互相不瞭解，但現在你們教我們你們的母語，我們才會知道、才能聽懂你們的話。
We didn’t know each other before, but now you teach us your mother tongue (Chinese) so we know and understand each other now.

S.FORM: malahang itaal ru nanu' yani a mana'aring miyan ni a mkibaq su kai' na tu'iya' ru kai' na sayhu' kai' na hulu'.
S.TRANSL: (政府)照顧泰雅人，所以我們開始學中文、客家話和河洛話(閩南語)。
(The government) takes care of Atayal people, and we start learning languages of Chinese, Hakka, and Southern Min.

S.FORM: nanu' yani ku maha kung maha u atu' na aqih bay ku inkahulan miyan ka.
S.TRANSL: 我一直在想，我們的發源真的很不好。
I’ve been thinking that our lives in the beginning are really not good.
